item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hate to say it, but it sort of feels like this was written for the title pun, without much reason beyond that.

I think the idea might have potential, and the technical execution is okay.

A no-vote from me, I'm afraid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't doing anything for me, the effect is too specific to be just "some random thing that happens lol" but there's no explanation. At the same time it's kind of boring and there's no hook or twist to make you like it. Not every skip has to be overly complex or profound, but the least they must be is interesting and this one is not. Downvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What you said only works if it left the readers with something like a fridge horror. A horrific realization of the things left unsaid. I felt none of it in this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean, there's not much more to know about it than that. If I explained what was happening then I would find it a lot less entertaining. I purposefully wrote it with weak protocols and wrote why it happens, otherwise, in my opinion, the article would lose its kick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get it that this SCP is Keter due to its potentially widespread effect. But it's boring, and it seemed like the Foundation were satisfied knowing very little about it, and decided to just label it Keter and then imposed a very weak containment protocol.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
god dammit i wanted this slot

In all seriousness though, this is really weird and fun and I just kinda like it overall. I remember this one eyeball fight club SCP from a while back which this kinda reminds me of, but I still just think this is a nice little scip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First Breakfast replace s ya with styrofoam puppets now sleep replaces you with cockroaches? Whats next?!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I was pretty confused
and not a bit of bit amused
It was probably his fault, my neighbor Anthony,
Why am I on a planet from the Andromeda Galaxy?

Series III … AGAIN?

Thank you very much to RockTeethMothEyes for the crit, I really appreciate it, plus ARD in IRC chat for recommending the title being "Beetle Roayle".

Image credits:

Weapons
Us map (edited by me.)

Hope y'all enjoy!

m͎o͎r͎e͎ ͎b͎y͎ ͎m͎e͎
